{{Quote|For nearly two years the standoff continued, until the besieged Techno Union engineers exploded from Foerost with warships that no one had imagined.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="NEC" />}}
The '''Battle of Foerost''' took place in [[21 BBY/Legends|20.1 BBY]], the second [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Since the war's [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|outset]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] had [[Foerost Siege|blockaded]] the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union's]] [[Foerost Shipyards]], hoping to prevent them from supplying [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. That changed when the beleaguered Techno Union [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] completed work on a new [[Warship/Legends|warship]]: the [[Bulwark Mark I]]. Commanded by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Dua Ningo/Legends|Dua Ningo]], an entire [[Fleet/Legends|armada]] of ''Bulwark''-class [[Battleship/Legends|battleships]], designated as the [[Bulwark Fleet]], [[Space warfare/Legends|breached]] Republic [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna's]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] of [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]]. The Bulwark Fleet's [[Foerost campaign|ensuing campaign]] against the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] forced the Republic to press into service a [[Victory Fleet|fleet]] of its own prototype [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer]], to hunt down Ningo's Bulwarks.

==Prelude==
[[Standard century/Legends|Centuries]] prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] purchased the [[Foerost Shipyards]],<ref name="HNN">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|53|business/13425_1.html|Techno Union Tightens Security at Foerost Shipyards}}</ref> a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Shipyard/Legends|construction facility]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] of [[Foerost/Legends|Foerost]]. Techno Union starship [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] soon developed a new design: the [[Bulwark Mark I]],<ref name="CSWE Foerost">{{CSWECite|I|284|Foerost}}</ref> a [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]]-long [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battlecruiser]] bristling with weaponry and heavy [[Armor/Legends|armor]]. Following the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|outbreak]] of the Clone Wars in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockaded]] Foerost, hoping to cut off the Techno Union shipyards from [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] supply lines and consequently send the facility into atrophy. The standoff, known as the [[Foerost Siege]], continued for nearly two full [[Standard year/Legends|years]] until the Techno Union designers decided to unveil their new creation.<ref name="NEC" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|With the old Sullustan Dua Ningo in charge of the armada, the Bulwark Fleet broke through the Republic's blockade and proceeded to smash military outposts throughout Coruscant's Sector Zero. To stop Ningo, the Republic launched its newest warship—the ''Victory''-class Star Destroyer—nearly six months early.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="NEC" />}}
In [[21 BBY/Legends|20.1 BBY]] ([[Great ReSynchronization|15:4]]), twenty-three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] into the war, the Techno Union engineers completed their work on the [[Bulwark Fleet]], an [[Fleet/Legends|armada]] of Bulwark Mark Is.<ref name="NEC" /> [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Dua Ningo/Legends|Dua Ningo]] of the [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederate Navy]] assumed command of the Bulwark Fleet, then blasted out of the Foerost Shipyards and [[Space warfare/Legends|broke through]] the Republic blockade, which was being overseen by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]]. Witnesses were left appalled by the Confederacy's unthinkable new [[Warship/Legends|warships]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Aftermath==
[[File:VSD-SWR.png|thumb|left|250px|The ''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer]]
With the Bulwarks liberated from Foerost, Ningo received orders to capture the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="CSWE Ningo">{{CSWECite|II|383|Ningo, Dua}}</ref> and his fleet [[Foerost campaign|crippled]] Republic outposts throughout [[Imperial Center Oversector|Sector Zero]] en route to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. To counter the Bulwarks, the Republic deployed its newest creation: the [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory I''-class Star Destroyer]],<ref name="NEC" /> designed by Republic engineer [[Walex Blissex/Legends|Walex Blissex]].<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> The product of the [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat]]/[[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili]] [[Victor Initiative Project]], the ''Victory I''-class [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] was launched almost six months ahead of schedule, with multiple ''Victory I''-class [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] forming the so-called [[Victory Fleet]] under the command of Republic [[Navy/Legends|Navy]] Captains Dodonna and [[Terrinald Screed/Legends|Terrinald Screed]]. The two [[Republic officer/Legends|Republic officers]] took their [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]] of Star [[Destroyer/Legends|Destroyers]] on what proved to be a memorable shakedown cruise, clashing with the Bulwark Fleet at the Core Worlds of [[Ixtlar/Legends|Ixtlar]], [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], and [[Basilisk]].<ref name="NEC" /> Following the conclusion of the crisis at the [[Battle of Anaxes (21 BBY)|Battle of Anaxes]], the Republic eventually recaptured Foerost from the Techno Union.<ref name="CSWE Foerost" />

The historic Battle of Foerost<ref name="TEA" /> was among the events that prominent [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[Historian/Legends|historian]] [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]] documented in his [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|chronicle of galactic history]] in [[36 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Battle of Foerost<ref name="TEA" /> was created by author [[Daniel Wallace]] for his [[2005]] compendium ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', which itself was framed as an [[in-universe]] narrative authored by Voren Na'al.<ref name="NEC" /> Wallace's setup of the battle, as well as the ensuing campaign between the Victory and Bulwark Fleets, took inspiration from material developed for earlier ''[[Star Wars]]'' works, including the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']]<ref name="NEC Endnotes">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/ref/f20051025/index.html?page=3|text=Endnotes for ''The New Essential Chronology'' Part Two: The Fall of the Republic}}</ref> and a [[2002]] news bulletin on the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]].<ref name="HNN" /> The battle was first identified as the Battle of Foerost in [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', which provided an official name for the battle,<ref name="TEA" />
